name: Manual
history: Balancing on the back two wheels while rolling. The skateboarding version of a wheelie. A fundamental skill that underpins countless combo tricks.
youtube_query: how to manual skateboard tutorial beginner

name: Nose Manual
history: Balancing on the front two wheels while rolling. Also called a nose wheelie. More difficult to control than a regular manual due to the nose weight.
youtube_query: how to nose manual skateboard tutorial

name: One Wheel Manual
history: Balancing on only one wheel (back right or back left) while rolling. Requires extreme balance and is considered a freestyle feat.

name: Nose Manual Kickflip Out
history: A nose manual that exits with a kickflip. A classic tech combo trick combining a sustained manual with a flip exit.
youtube_query: nose manual kickflip out skateboard tutorial

name: Manual Kickflip Out
history: A manual that ends with a kickflip. One of the most common manual combo tricks in street skating.

name: Manual Heelflip Out
history: A manual exited with a heelflip. Requires maintaining balance through the manual before executing the flip.

name: Nose Manual Heelflip Out
history: A nose manual that exits with a heelflip. A technical combo requiring balance and precise foot placement.

name: Manual 360 Flip Out
history: A manual with a 360 flip (tre flip) exit. An impressive and technical combo trick.

name: Nose Manual to Manual
history: Transitioning from a nose manual to a regular manual within the same combo. Requires extremely precise weight shifting.
youtube_query: nose manual to manual skateboard tutorial

name: Kickflip Manual
history: A kickflip into a manual. The flip trick sets up the back-wheel balance position.

name: Heelflip Manual
history: A heelflip into a manual. A classic tech street combo trick.

name: 360 Flip Manual
history: A 360 flip (tre flip) into a manual. One of the most technical manual entry tricks.

name: Varial Kickflip Manual
history: A varial kickflip into a manual. An intermediate-level tech combo.

name: Casper Stall
history: The board is balanced on its nose (or tail) with the grip tape facing up while the skater's foot holds it in place. Named after Bobby 'Casper' Boyden.

name: Rail Stand
history: The board is balanced vertically on one of its rails (side edges) while the skater stands on it. A classic Rodney Mullen freestyle trick.
youtube_query: rail stand skateboard freestyle trick Rodney Mullen

name: Pogo
history: The board is stood on its tail and the skater bounces on it like a pogo stick. A classic freestyle trick popularized in the early 1980s.

name: Primo
history: The board is balanced on its side (one rail and one wheel) while the skater balances on it. Named after professional skater Primo Desiderio.

name: Headstand
history: The skater balances on their head on the board while rolling. An extremely rare and gymnastic trick from the early days of freestyle skating.

name: 360 Shove-it Casper
history: A 360 shove-it that lands in a Casper (upside-down board) position. Combines a spin trick with a balance trick in one fluid motion.

name: One-Foot Manual
history: A manual performed with only one foot on the board. A freestyle trick requiring exceptional balance.

name: No-Handed 50-50
history: Performing a 50-50 grind without using hands for balance or assistance. A refined version of the standard grind emphasizing pure skill.

name: Helipop
history: A nollie 360 backside shove-it combined with a flip. A freestyle trick that Rodney Mullen brought into street skating.

name: Casper Slide
history: Sliding on the grip tape with the board upside down (Casper position). An advanced freestyle sliding trick.

name: Anti Casper
history: A variation of the Casper where the board is flipped the other direction. An obscure freestyle trick rarely performed outside of dedicated freestyle competitions.

name: Endover
history: A continuous series of 180-degree pivots alternating between nose and tail, creating a walking motion. A fundamental freestyle move from the 1960s.

name: Spinner
history: A flatground 360-degree body spin performed without lifting the board off the ground. An early freestyle fundamental trick.

name: Coffin
history: The skater lies flat on their back on the board while rolling. A classic old-school trick that requires balance and fearlessness.

name: Footplant
history: The skater plants one or both feet on the ground temporarily while doing a trick. A broad family of tricks popularized in both freestyle and early street skating.

name: Darkslide Manual
history: Balancing in a manual while the board is flipped upside down (grip tape down). An incredibly difficult trick blending Rodney Mullen's darkslide concept with manual balance.

name: Switchfoot 360 Shove-it
history: A 360 shove-it where the skater switches from regular to switch stance during the trick. A Rodney Mullen specialty combining footwork with board tricks.
youtube_query: switchfoot 360 shove it skateboard Rodney Mullen

name: 360 Casper
history: The board rotates 360 degrees while in Casper (upside-down) position. An advanced freestyle combination trick.

name: G-Turn
history: A frontside 360-degree turn performed by pumping the board with alternating weight shifts rather than an ollie. A flowing old-school freestyle maneuver.

name: Ollie Impossible Shove-it
history: An impossible combined with a shove-it. The board wraps around the foot and rotates simultaneously. An extremely obscure Rodney Mullen-style combination.

name: Handstand
history: The skater performs a handstand on the board while rolling. A gymnastic trick from the earliest days of freestyle skateboarding.

name: Sit Down
history: The skater sits down on the board while rolling, then stands back up. A casual-looking trick requiring precise balance.

name: Gorilla Grip Walk
history: The skater grabs the board with both hands overhand while rolling and walks alongside the board. An old school freestyle exhibition move.

name: Spacewalk
history: A continuous slalom-style weaving trick where the skater shifts weight side to side rhythmically. Associated with freestyle and longboard dancing.

name: Tiger Claw
history: The back foot kicks the tail of the board up, the front hand catches it mid-air, and then the board is placed back under the feet. A unique Rodney Mullen freestyle trick.
youtube_query: tiger claw skateboard trick Rodney Mullen

name: Kick Nose Manual
history: The skater kicks the board forward into a nose manual position without an ollie entry. A flow-based freestyle manual technique.

name: Foot Drag
history: One foot drags on the ground to slow down or control direction while the other foot remains on the board. Used for speed control and style.

name: Two-Foot Walk
history: The skater alternates between placing each foot on the ground while the board rolls underneath. A walking illusion trick.
youtube_query: two foot walk skateboard freestyle trick

name: 540 Shove-it
history: The board rotates 540 degrees (one and a half rotations) while the skater ollies. A more extreme version of the 360 shove-it.

name: 720 Shove-it
history: The board rotates 720 degrees (two full rotations) from a shove-it motion. Extremely rare and requires significant height to complete.

name: Ollie One-Footer
history: After an ollie, one foot is kicked off the board while the other foot maintains contact. A stylish old-school extension trick.
youtube_query: ollie one footer skateboard trick tutorial

name: Ollie Impossible to Manual
history: An impossible trick that lands in a manual. A Rodney Mullen-inspired combination of two of his signature tricks.

name: Nose Manual 360 Flip Out
history: A nose manual that exits with a 360 flip. One of the most technically demanding manual combo tricks.

name: Kickflip to Noseslide
history: A kickflip that lands in a noseslide position. Requires the board to flip and then the nose to lock onto the obstacle.

name: Heelflip to Noseslide
history: A heelflip entry into a noseslide. The heel flick must be controlled enough to align the nose for the slide.

name: 360 Flip to Boardslide
history: A 360 flip that lands in a boardslide position. The combination of the spinning flip and the perpendicular landing makes this extremely difficult.
